alerts.title: US and Iran reach agreement in principle to reopen Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: NYT cites a US official confirming Washington and Tehran have agreed in principle to a deal reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with PBS and BBC quoting Trump that the broader Iran deal is 'largely negotiated.' Times of Israel reports the Iranian-circulated text stipulates mutual non-attack between the US/allies and Iran/its allies. Brent crude has eased to ~$100, though the nuclear stockpile question remains unresolved.

alerts.title: Houthi missile strike on Israel revives Red Sea shipping threat
alerts.summary: AP and Times of Israel report a Houthi missile attack on Israel has stoked fears of renewed Ansar Allah strikes on Red Sea commercial shipping, with BBC and Global Security Review flagging escalation risk to the global economy. The strike comes as USS Eisenhower, Bataan, Carney, Mason and Laboon remain concentrated in the southern Red Sea, and proxy status for the Houthis is logged as 'Elevated — Multiple Recent Attacks.'

alerts.title: Russia reportedly fires two Oreshnik IRBMs with MIRVs at Kyiv region
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator (~52K views) reports Russia launched two hypersonic Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Ukraine, with footage showing multiple independently targetable reentry vehicles impacting the Kyiv region. Rybar separately discusses an Oreshnik strike on Bila Tserkva using kinetic (non-explosive) warheads, noting reduced public effect. The system is normally nuclear-capable and near-impossible to intercept; treat strike details as pending Western confirmation.

alerts.title: Israeli intelligence warns Tehran may be preparing a surprise attack
alerts.summary: The Jerusalem Post reports Israeli intelligence officials assess that Iran could be planning a surprise attack even as Trump-Iran negotiations advance, a warning that runs counter to the 'largely negotiated' deal narrative. Haaretz separately reports religious norms are preventing deployment of a key IDF system at a Lebanon post, suggesting unresolved readiness gaps along the northern front.

alerts.title: GOP hawks publicly break with Trump over emerging Iran deal
alerts.summary: Forbes and Middle East Eye report a 'fiery' Republican backlash to the emerging Iran framework, with US sentiment data showing surging mentions of Senator Tillis, Hegseth, Gabbard and Rubio alongside 'criticism,' 'against' and 'war' in the top trending terms (US sentiment 59% negative). Middle East Eye characterizes the deal as a 'nightmare for Israel' per hawkish critics, signaling potential domestic political resistance to ratification.

analysis_note: The theater is bifurcating: a US-Iran Strait of Hormuz deal is now 'agreed in principle' and oil has retreated to ~$100, but a fresh Houthi strike on Israel, an Israeli warning of a possible Iranian surprise attack, and mounting GOP opposition keep threat level at 9/10 pending a signed text and resolution of the enriched uranium question.
